vineyards.name: Marchesi Antinori
vineyards.address: Piazza degli Antinori 3, 50123 Firenze, Italy
vineyards.classification: Chianti Classico DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.owner: Antinori family (Marchese Piero Antinori, chairman emeritus; Albiera Antinori, president)
vineyards.tasting_program: Antinori nel Chianti Classico cellar at Bargino offers visits, tastings and the Rinuccio 1180 restaurant; multi-tier tasting programmes by reservation.
vineyards.description: The Antinori family has produced wine in Tuscany since 1385. Anchored by the Tignanello estate in Chianti Classico, the parent house also owns Bolgheri's Guado al Tasso under a separate producer slug.

vineyards.name: Castello di Ama
vineyards.address: Localita Ama, 53013 Gaiole in Chianti, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar tour, hamlet walk, art collection and tasting flight bookable through the estate; the property hosts a contemporary art programme with installations by Anish Kapoor and Louise Bourgeois.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1972 in the hamlet of Ama at Gaiole in Chianti, the estate produces single-vineyard Gran Selezione bottlings from Bellavista and La Casuccia and pairs the wine programme with a contemporary art collection.

vineyards.name: Felsina
vineyards.address: Via del Chianti 101, 53019 Castelnuovo Berardenga, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tasting flights by appointment; the Rancia single-vineyard Gran Selezione is the estate's flagship.
vineyards.description: Castelnuovo Berardenga estate established 1966 by Domenico Poggiali. Sangiovese-focused with the Rancia Gran Selezione and the IGT Fontalloro forming the core of the range.

vineyards.name: Fontodi
vineyards.address: Localita San Leolino 89, 50022 Panzano in Chianti, Firenze, Italy
vineyards.classification: Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment in the Conca d'Oro amphitheatre of Panzano; organic since 1990 and certified by ICEA.
vineyards.description: Panzano estate in the Conca d'Oro amphitheatre, owned by the Manetti family since 1968 and ICEA-organic certified since 1990. Flaccianello della Pieve is the flagship Sangiovese IGT.

verified.address_quoted: Localita San Leolino 89, 50020 Panzano in Chianti (FI)

vineyards.name: Isole e Olena
vineyards.address: Localita Isole 1, 50021 Barberino Tavarnelle, Firenze, Italy
vineyards.classification: Chianti Classico DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.owner: EPI Group (Christopher Descours), acquired from the De Marchi family in 2022
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment; Cepparello (100% Sangiovese IGT) is the long-running flagship from the De Marchi era.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1956 by the De Marchi family; the EPI Group acquired the estate in 2022. The Cepparello pure Sangiovese IGT remains the flagship alongside the Chianti Classico and the Syrah from the Collezione De Marchi range.

vineyards.name: San Felice
vineyards.address: Localita San Felice, 53019 Castelnuovo Berardenga, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione and Toscana IGT
vineyards.owner: Allianz Group (insurance investor); historic property of the Grisaldi del Taja family
vineyards.tasting_program: The Borgo San Felice resort hosts cellar tours, tasting flights and a vineyard experience programme; the property includes a Relais & Chateaux hotel.
vineyards.description: Castelnuovo Berardenga estate combining a Relais & Chateaux resort with a Chianti Classico programme that recovered the indigenous Pugnitello grape from near-extinction.

vineyards.name: Castello di Volpaia
vineyards.address: Localita Volpaia, 53017 Radda in Chianti, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione
vineyards.tasting_program: The fortified medieval hamlet of Volpaia houses the cellar across the village buildings; tastings, cellar walks and Bottega lunches by booking.
vineyards.description: ICEA-organic certified estate on a fortified hamlet at 600 metres in Radda in Chianti, with the cellar dispersed through medieval buildings owned by the Mascheroni Stianti family.

vineyards.name: Querciabella
vineyards.address: Via di Barbiano 17, 50022 Greve in Chianti, Firenze, Italy
vineyards.classification: Chianti Classico DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings and estate visits by appointment; the entire farming operation is Demeter biodynamic and the cellar uses no animal products in vinification.
vineyards.description: Greve in Chianti estate founded 1974 by the Castiglioni family. Demeter-certified biodynamic with vegan vinification practices; Camartina is the long-running Sangiovese-Cabernet IGT.

verified.address_quoted: Via di Barbiano 17, 50022 Greve in Chianti (FI)

vineyards.name: Castell'in Villa
vineyards.address: Localita Castell'in Villa, 53019 Castelnuovo Berardenga, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Chianti Classico DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.winemaker: Coralia Pignatelli della Leonessa with Vittorio Fiore consulting
vineyards.tasting_program: Estate visits and tasting flights by appointment; lunch available in the borgo with prior reservation.
vineyards.description: Castelnuovo Berardenga property owned since 1968 by Principessa Coralia Pignatelli, producing some of Chianti Classico's longest-lived traditionally-styled Riservas.

vineyards.name: Riecine
vineyards.address: Localita Riecine, 53013 Gaiole in Chianti, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Small-group estate tastings by reservation; vegan-vinified wines.
vineyards.description: Tiny Gaiole estate of 13 hectares founded in 1971 by Englishman John Dunkley. Organic since 2014, vegan vinification, and now producing 100% Sangiovese under the La Gioia Toscana IGT flagship.

vineyards.name: Castello di Querceto
vineyards.address: Via Alessandro Francois 2, 50022 Greve in Chianti, Firenze, Italy
vineyards.classification: Chianti Classico DOCG Gran Selezione and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar tours and tasting flights from the medieval castle property; on-site agriturismo accommodation.
vineyards.description: Francois family estate in Greve in Chianti dating to 1897, set around a medieval castle. Il Picchio Gran Selezione anchors a Sangiovese-led range alongside Super Tuscan IGT bottlings.

verified.address_quoted: Via Alessandro Francois 2, 50022 Greve in Chianti (FI)

vineyards.name: Biondi-Santi
vineyards.address: Villa Greppo 183, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Brunello di Montalcino DOCG
vineyards.owner: EPI Group (Christopher Descours), acquired from the Biondi Santi family in 2017
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar tours of the historic Greppo property by appointment; rare vintages tasting on request.
vineyards.description: The founding estate of Brunello di Montalcino; Ferruccio Biondi Santi bottled the first 1888 vintage. Now owned by EPI Group with the historic Greppo Sangiovese Grosso vines as the core.

vineyards.name: Case Basse di Gianfranco Soldera
vineyards.address: Localita Case Basse, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy
vineyards.neighborhood: Brunello di Montalcino DOCG (now bottled as Toscana IGT)
vineyards.classification: Toscana IGT (estate withdrew from Brunello DOCG in 2013)
vineyards.owner: Soldera family (children of Gianfranco Soldera, who passed in 2019)
vineyards.winemaker: Mauro Fastelli (cellar master under Gianfranco Soldera)
vineyards.tasting_program: Not currently open to the public; allocation-only sales via mailing list.
vineyards.description: Cult Montalcino estate founded 1972 by Gianfranco Soldera. Withdrew from Brunello DOCG in 2013 after a cellar sabotage incident; bottles continue as Toscana IGT from the original Intistieti and Case Basse vineyards.

vineyards.name: Casanova di Neri
vineyards.address: Podere Fiesole, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Brunello di Montalcino DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.owner: Neri family (Giacomo Neri, owner and winemaker)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits, vineyard walks and vertical tastings of Tenuta Nuova and Cerretalto by appointment.
vineyards.description: Family estate founded 1971, with the single-vineyard Cerretalto and the Tenuta Nuova selection driving the reputation. Wine Spectator named the 2001 Tenuta Nuova its 2006 Wine of the Year.

vineyards.name: Castello Banfi
vineyards.address: Castello di Poggio alle Mura, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Brunello di Montalcino DOCG, Sant'Antimo DOC and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Castello Banfi houses a Relais & Chateaux hotel, restaurants, an enoteca and the Banfi glass-and-wine museum; cellar tours and tasting flights run daily.
vineyards.description: Single largest Brunello estate at 850 hectares of vineyards inside Montalcino. Mariani family-owned since 1978 and home to the Castello di Poggio alle Mura medieval fortress and Relais hotel.

verified.address_quoted: Castello di Poggio alle Mura, 53024 Montalcino (SI)

vineyards.name: Tenuta Caparzo
vineyards.address: Localita Caparzo 6, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Brunello di Montalcino DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by reservation; the Vigna La Casa cru is the historical flagship.
vineyards.description: Founded 1970 in the cooler northern Montalcino. The Vigna La Casa single-vineyard Brunello has been bottled since 1977; estate has been organic since 2014.

vineyards.name: Poggio Antico
vineyards.address: Localita Poggio Antico, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Brunello di Montalcino DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Estate visits, tastings and on-site restaurant overlooking the southern Montalcino vineyards.
vineyards.description: High-elevation estate at 450-500 metres in the southern Montalcino. Certified organic with ICEA, producing structured age-worthy Brunello and the Altero IGT Sangiovese.

vineyards.name: Il Poggione
vineyards.address: Localita Monteano, 53024 Sant'Angelo in Colle, Montalcino, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Brunello di Montalcino DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Estate tours, cellar visits and tasting flights at Sant'Angelo in Colle by appointment.
vineyards.description: Franceschi-family estate founded 1890 in Sant'Angelo in Colle, southern Montalcino. Vigna Paganelli Riserva uses the estate's oldest Sangiovese Grosso vines planted in 1964.

verified.address_quoted: Localita Monteano, 53024 Sant'Angelo in Colle, Montalcino (SI)

vineyards.name: Mastrojanni
vineyards.address: Podere Loreto e San Pio, 53024 Castelnuovo dell'Abate, Montalcino, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Brunello di Montalcino DOCG
vineyards.tasting_program: Estate tours and cellar visits by appointment; the southeast-facing Vigna Loreto cru is the property's calling card.
vineyards.description: Castelnuovo dell'Abate estate founded in 1975 and acquired by the Illy family in 2008. The Vigna Loreto and Schiena d'Asino crus are among Montalcino's most idiosyncratic Sangiovese expressions.

verified.address_quoted: Podere Loreto e San Pio, 53024 Castelnuovo dell'Abate (SI)

vineyards.name: Argiano
vineyards.address: Sant'Angelo in Colle, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Brunello di Montalcino DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.owner: Andre Esteves (Brazilian financier) with the Lovatelli family
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar tours, tasting flights and the Casa di Argiano dining experience at the Renaissance villa.
vineyards.description: Renaissance villa estate at Sant'Angelo in Colle dating to 1580. Wine Spectator named the 2018 Brunello its Wine of the Year for 2023, the first Italian wine to claim that ranking in two decades.

vineyards.name: Col d'Orcia
vineyards.address: Localita Sant'Angelo in Colle, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Brunello di Montalcino DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Estate tours, cellar visits and tastings by reservation; one of the largest organic-certified estates in Italy.
vineyards.description: Cinzano family estate at Sant'Angelo in Colle, 145 hectares of vines under one of Italy's largest single-block organic conversions (certified ICEA in 2013). Poggio al Vento Riserva is the historic single-vineyard Brunello.

vineyards.name: Avignonesi
vineyards.address: Via Colonica 1, 53045 Valiano di Montepulciano, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG, Vin Santo di Montepulciano DOC and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar tours, Vin Santo vinsantaia tastings and estate lunches by reservation; certified biodynamic by Demeter.
vineyards.description: Montepulciano estate acquired by Belgian shipowner Virginie Saverys in 2009 and converted to Demeter-certified biodynamics. The Occhio di Pernice Vin Santo from Sangiovese remains a benchmark of the style.

vineyards.name: Poderi Boscarelli
vineyards.address: Via di Montenero 28, 53045 Cervognano, Montepulciano, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.winemaker: Maurizio Castelli (consulting) with Luca De Ferrari
vineyards.tasting_program: Boutique cellar visits and tastings by appointment; the Vigna del Nocio single-vineyard is the estate's calling card.
vineyards.description: De Ferrari Corradi family estate at Cervognano, 16 hectares of high-density Prugnolo Gentile. The Vigna del Nocio Vino Nobile, from a single 1968-planted vineyard, is the flagship.

vineyards.name: Salcheto
vineyards.address: Via di Villa Bianca 15, 53045 Montepulciano, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.winemaker: Michele Manelli with Federico Curtaz consulting
vineyards.tasting_program: Tours of the off-grid energy-independent cellar, tastings and a restaurant overlooking Montepulciano; certified organic.
vineyards.description: Off-grid solar-powered cellar at Montepulciano, certified organic by CCPB. Salco Evoluzione is the Riserva flagship; the estate publishes a carbon-footprint declaration on every label.

vineyards.name: Poliziano
vineyards.address: Via Fontago 1, 53045 Montepulciano Stazione, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Vino Nobile di Montepulciano DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar tours and tasting flights by appointment; the Asinone single-vineyard Vino Nobile is the flagship.
vineyards.description: Carletti family estate founded 1961 at the foot of Montepulciano. The Asinone single-vineyard Vino Nobile, named after the donkey-shaped hillside, is among the most consistently reviewed wines of the DOCG.

vineyards.name: Tenuta San Guido
vineyards.address: Localita Le Capanne 27, 57020 Bolgheri, Livorno, Italy
vineyards.classification: Bolgheri Sassicaia DOC (single-estate DOC, established 1994)
vineyards.owner: Incisa della Rocchetta family (Marchese Nicolo Incisa della Rocchetta, president)
vineyards.winemaker: Carlo Paoli (estate manager) with Graziana Grassini consulting
vineyards.tasting_program: Strictly limited visits; the estate prioritises trade and allocation customers. Public access is rare.
vineyards.description: The Bolgheri estate where Mario Incisa della Rocchetta planted Bordeaux cuttings in 1944. First released commercially as Sassicaia 1968; awarded its own Bolgheri Sassicaia DOC in 1994, the only single-estate DOC in Italy.

vineyards.name: Tenuta dell'Ornellaia
vineyards.address: Localita Ornellaia 191, 57022 Bolgheri, Livorno, Italy
vineyards.classification: Bolgheri Superiore DOC and Toscana IGT
vineyards.winemaker: Marco Balsimelli (estate director); Olga Fusari, head winemaker
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar tours, vineyard walks and tutored tastings by appointment; the Vendemmia d'Artista label programme is on display at the cellar.
vineyards.description: Bolgheri estate founded by Lodovico Antinori in 1981 and fully owned by Frescobaldi since 2005. Bordeaux-cloned reds with Masseto and Ornellaia as flagships; the estate is ICEA-organic certified.

vineyards.name: Masseto
vineyards.address: Localita Ornellaia 191, 57022 Bolgheri, Livorno, Italy
vineyards.classification: Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits to the dedicated Masseto cellar by very limited appointment; one of Italy's hardest-to-secure cellar tours.
vineyards.description: A 7-hectare blue-clay Merlot parcel inside the Ornellaia estate. First released as a separate brand in 1986. Now operates with its own dedicated cellar opened in 2020; widely considered Italy's most prestigious Merlot.

vineyards.name: Tenuta Guado al Tasso
vineyards.address: Localita Belvedere 116, 57020 Bolgheri, Livorno, Italy
vineyards.classification: Bolgheri Superiore DOC and Toscana IGT
vineyards.owner: Marchesi Antinori (the Antinori family's Bolgheri estate, separate producer slug from Marchesi Antinori in Chianti Classico)
vineyards.winemaker: Renzo Cotarella (Antinori chief winemaker); Pierluigi Talamo, estate director
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits, vineyard walks and the Osteria Magona village kitchen at Bolgheri; bookings via the Antinori hospitality desk.
vineyards.description: Antinori's Bolgheri estate, established as a standalone vineyard project in 1990 from the family's 1932 land purchase. 320 hectares with the Guado al Tasso Superiore as flagship and the rare 100% Cabernet Franc Matarocchio.

vineyards.name: Le Macchiole
vineyards.address: Via Bolgherese 189A, 57022 Bolgheri, Livorno, Italy
vineyards.classification: Bolgheri DOC and Toscana IGT
vineyards.owner: Cinzia Merli and family (widow of co-founder Eugenio Campolmi)
vineyards.winemaker: Cinzia Merli with Luca d'Attoma consulting
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tutored tastings by appointment; vegan vinification practices used since 2014.
vineyards.description: Family estate founded 1983 by Eugenio Campolmi; Cinzia Merli has led the property since his death in 2002. Single-varietal Cabernet Franc (Paleo), Merlot (Messorio) and Syrah (Scrio) bottlings; CCPB-organic certified.

vineyards.name: Grattamacco
vineyards.address: Localita Lungagnano 129, 57022 Castagneto Carducci, Livorno, Italy
vineyards.classification: Bolgheri Superiore DOC and Toscana IGT
vineyards.winemaker: Luca Marrone with Maurizio Castelli consulting
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits, vineyard walks and tastings by appointment; certified organic since 2014.
vineyards.description: The second estate to release a Bolgheri red after Sassicaia, founded 1977. Acquired by Claudio Tipa's ColleMassari group in 2002; CCPB-organic certified since 2014.

vineyards.name: Michele Satta
vineyards.address: Localita Casone Ugolino 23, 57022 Castagneto Carducci, Livorno, Italy
vineyards.classification: Bolgheri DOC and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar tours and tasting flights by appointment; Michele Satta personally hosts on advance booking.
vineyards.description: Bolgheri's enduring artisan: Michele Satta arrived in 1974 and bottled his first wine in 1983. Notable for keeping a 100% Sangiovese (Cavaliere) on the Bordeaux-dominated coast.

vineyards.name: Tenuta di Capezzana
vineyards.address: Via Capezzana 100, 59015 Carmignano, Prato, Italy
vineyards.classification: Carmignano DOCG and Vin Santo di Carmignano DOC
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar tours, Vin Santo vinsantaia visits, vineyard walks and on-site agriturismo accommodation; certified organic.
vineyards.description: Contini Bonacossi family estate at Carmignano with vine-growing records back to 804 AD on the property. Wine production formalised by the Contini Bonacossi family in 1920; certified organic and a Carmignano DOCG benchmark.

vineyards.name: Fattoria Le Pupille
vineyards.address: Localita Piagge del Maiano 92A, 58051 Magliano in Toscana, Grosseto, Italy
vineyards.classification: Morellino di Scansano DOCG, Maremma Toscana DOC and Toscana IGT
vineyards.winemaker: Luca d'Attoma (consulting) with Cosimo Cossutti, estate enologist
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar tours, vineyard walks and tasting flights by reservation; Saffredi is the long-running Super Tuscan flagship.
vineyards.description: Maremma estate built by Elisabetta Geppetti from her 1985 inheritance into the region's reference producer. Saffredi, the Cabernet-Merlot-Alicante IGT first bottled in 1987, anchors the range alongside Morellino di Scansano.

verified.address_quoted: Localita Piagge del Maiano 92A, 58051 Magliano in Toscana (GR)

vineyards.name: Rocca di Frassinello
vineyards.address: Localita Giuncarico, 58023 Gavorrano, Grosseto, Italy
vineyards.classification: Maremma Toscana DOC and Toscana IGT
vineyards.owner: Castellare di Castellina (Paolo Panerai) in joint venture with Domaines Barons de Rothschild (Lafite)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tours of the Renzo Piano-designed cellar, vineyard walks and tasting flights by reservation; the cellar's underground barrel hall is a destination in itself.
vineyards.description: Joint venture between Castellare di Castellina and Domaines Barons de Rothschild (Lafite), housed in a Renzo Piano-designed cellar at Gavorrano. Baffonero, a 100% Merlot, is the prestige bottling.

vineyards.name: Panizzi
vineyards.address: Localita Santa Margherita 34, 53037 San Gimignano, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Vernaccia di San Gimignano DOCG, Chianti Colli Senesi DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.owner: Simone Niccolai (acquired from the Panizzi family in 2010)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar tours, tasting flights and an on-site museum dedicated to Vernaccia; guided visits at the Santa Margherita property.
vineyards.description: San Gimignano's leading Vernaccia specialist, founded 1979 by Giovanni Panizzi and acquired by Simone Niccolai in 2010. The Riserva ages in a mix of stainless steel, tonneau and acacia wood.

vineyards.name: Castello di Nipozzano
vineyards.address: Via di Nipozzano 22, 50060 Pelago, Firenze, Italy
vineyards.classification: Chianti Rufina DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Castle tours, cellar visits, vineyard tastings and lunch in the medieval estate by reservation; one of the Frescobaldi estate-visit anchors.
vineyards.description: Frescobaldi's Rufina estate, in the family since 1000 AD and on its current 250-hectare scale since the mid-19th century. Montesodi single-vineyard Sangiovese is the long-running flagship.

vineyards.name: Frescobaldi CastelGiocondo
vineyards.address: Localita CastelGiocondo, 53024 Montalcino, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Brunello di Montalcino DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Estate tours, Brunello cellar visits and tasting flights by reservation; Luce della Vite is bottled separately on a neighbouring property.
vineyards.description: Frescobaldi's Montalcino estate, acquired in 1989. 235 hectares of Sangiovese Grosso in the western reaches of the appellation; Ripe al Convento is the Riserva from oldest vines.

vineyards.name: Frescobaldi Tenuta di Castiglioni
vineyards.address: Via di Castiglioni 4, 50025 Montespertoli, Firenze, Italy
vineyards.classification: Chianti Colli Fiorentini DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits, vineyard walks and tasting flights at the Castiglioni manor; Tenuta di Castiglioni is the historical Frescobaldi family property.
vineyards.description: The Frescobaldi family's oldest property, in continuous family ownership since the 11th century. 110 hectares of vines in Montespertoli; Giramonte is the Bordeaux-blended IGT flagship.

vineyards.name: Frescobaldi Tenuta di Pomino
vineyards.address: Via di Petrognano 19, 50060 Pomino, Pelago, Firenze, Italy
vineyards.classification: Pomino DOC and Toscana IGT
vineyards.tasting_program: Estate visits and tastings by reservation at the high-altitude Pomino property; the cellar specialises in white-grape work unusual for the region.
vineyards.description: High-elevation Frescobaldi estate at Pomino (550 to 700 metres) bordering Chianti Rufina. Pinot Nero and Chardonnay vineyards date to 1855; Benefizio Riserva is one of Tuscany's pioneering barrel-aged Chardonnays.

vineyards.name: Fattoria Selvapiana
vineyards.address: Via Selvapiana 43, 50068 Rufina, Firenze, Italy
vineyards.classification: Chianti Rufina DOCG and Toscana IGT
vineyards.winemaker: Federico Giuntini Masseti with Franco Bernabei consulting
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits, vineyard walks and tastings by appointment; the Bucerchiale single-vineyard is the long-running flagship.
vineyards.description: Family-owned Chianti Rufina estate since 1827, certified organic by ICEA. The Bucerchiale single-vineyard Riserva is among Tuscany's most consistently age-worthy Sangiovese under €40.

vineyards.name: Tua Rita
vineyards.address: Localita Notri 81, 57028 Suvereto, Livorno, Italy
vineyards.neighborhood: Suvereto DOCG (Val di Cornia coastal area)
vineyards.classification: Toscana IGT, Val di Cornia DOC and Suvereto DOCG
vineyards.winemaker: Stefano Frascolla with Luca d'Attoma consulting
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar tours, vineyard walks and tasting flights by appointment at Suvereto, just south of Bolgheri.
vineyards.description: Suvereto estate founded 1984; Redigaffi, a 100% Merlot, scored 100 points from Wine Advocate for the 2000 vintage and put the Val di Cornia on the Super Tuscan map.

vineyards.name: Fattoria Le Fonti
vineyards.address: Localita Le Fonti 41, 53036 Poggibonsi, Siena, Italy
vineyards.classification: Chianti Classico DOCG and Vin Santo del Chianti Classico DOC
vineyards.winemaker: Vicky Schmitt-Vitali with Stefano Porcinai consulting
vineyards.tasting_program: Small-group estate tastings and cellar visits by appointment; agriturismo rooms available; certified organic.
vineyards.description: Small Chianti Classico estate at Poggibonsi run by Vicky Schmitt-Vitali since 1999. Certified ICEA-organic; the Vin Santo del Chianti Classico is aged 10 years in caratelli.
